pattern: A single pass that extends a run whenever the next character is exactly one alphabet step up tracks the longest consecutive-letter stretch in linear time.
hints: Walk through the string comparing each character with the one before it.
A run continues only when the current letter is exactly one position after the previous letter in the alphabet.
Keep a current run length, reset it to one whenever the step breaks, and remember the maximum seen.

edge_inputs.description: scattered letters cap the run at two
single character at end of alphabet
wraparound z to a does not continue
all identical letters give run of one
descending letters never form a run
run resets then a longer run appears

stress.description: long random lowercase string biased toward steps to create varied runs
stress.note: frequent +1 steps build runs that the linear scan must measure
